name: pnp-sp-dev-spfx-web-parts-react-legallens
title: LegalLens – AI-Powered Contract Management & E-Signature
shortDescription: AI-powered contract management and e-signature platform built on SharePoint Online with Azure AI Foundry integration, multi-signer workflows, and a vendor-facing signing flow that requires no SharePoint access.
longDescription: LegalLens is a SharePoint Framework (SPFx) web part that delivers an end-to-end AI-powered contract management and e-signature solution built on SharePoint Online. It reads contracts directly from SharePoint document libraries and enriches them with AI-driven classification, risk scoring (0–100), and key clause extraction powered by Azure AI Foundry and Azure Document Intelligence. The Contract Library view gives users a single place to browse all contracts, review metadata, monitor compliance status, and act on AI insights. The built-in Q&A Agent lets users ask natural-language questions about any contract in their preferred language — English is always included, with additional languages configurable via web part properties. The E-Signature module supports multi-signer workflows: internal staff sign directly inside the web part, while external vendors receive a secure one-time link and sign through a standalone static page (sign.html) backed by Azure Functions — no SharePoint login required for external parties. Documents are previewed page-by-page for PDFs, inline for plain-text files, and offered as a download for Word documents. Every signing event is timestamped, logged to an audit trail, and monitored via Application Insights. A PowerShell setup script automates the creation of the Contracts library, Signed Documents library, and Signature Tokens list with all required columns.

references.name: Build your first SharePoint client-side web part
references.description: Client-side web parts are client-side components that run in the context of a SharePoint page. Client-side web parts can be deployed to SharePoint environments that support the SharePoint Framework.

references.description: Learn how to build, evaluate, and deploy generative AI models and applications using Azure AI Foundry.

references.name: Azure Document Intelligence
references.description: Azure Document Intelligence (formerly Form Recognizer) uses AI to extract text, key-value pairs, tables, and structures from documents.

references.description: Learn how to develop and deploy Azure Functions using Node.js for serverless backend logic.

references.description: Use the Microsoft Graph Files API to access and manage files stored in SharePoint and OneDrive.
